problem.title: Coin Change
problem.description: You are given an integer array coins representing coins of different denominations and an integer amount representing a total amount of money. Return the fewest number of coins that you need to make up that amount. If that amount of money cannot be made up by any combination of the coins, return -1.
assumptions: You have an infinite supply of each coin.
Return -1 if the amount cannot be made.

solutions.title: Solution 1 – Dynamic Programming [✓ Interviewer Accepted]
solutions.description: Build a bottom-up DP table where dp[i] holds the minimum number of coins to make amount i. For each amount, try every coin denomination and take the minimum of using that coin plus the optimal solution for the remaining amount. Initializing dp[0]=0 and the rest to a sentinel value larger than any valid answer drives the recurrence correctly.

solutions.title: Solution 2 – Top-Down Memoization [✓ Interviewer Accepted]
solutions.description: Recursively compute the minimum coins needed for amount, memoizing results. For each amount, try every coin and take 1 + minCoins(amount - coin). This top-down approach is often easier to derive in an interview and produces the same O(amount * coins) complexity.
